milestones.title: First public Docker image path documented
milestones.summary: Added the pre-alpha Docker image path sleepiestmario/whacksmacker:latest.

milestones.title: Current Docker run command documented
milestones.summary: Recorded the Docker command with non-root user, HOME=/data, data-volume, --data-dir, and --catalogue setup so review progress and package data are not created as root-owned files.

milestones.title: WhackSmacker packaging VM role clarified
milestones.summary: Promoted gentoo-wsm-validate from a Docker validation VM into the main WhackSmacker packaging/validation VM, while keeping gentoo-base as the clean reusable Gentoo base VM.

milestones.title: Host-side VM automation fixed
milestones.summary: Updated the VM automation path to use system libvirt explicitly with virsh -c qemu:///system.

milestones.title: Packaging roadmap established
milestones.summary: Set the current path as Docker first, Gentoo validation next, Arch/AUR after that, then Debian/Ubuntu .deb, RPM-family packages, and eventually a generic Linux tarball or AppImage.

milestones.title: Review UI prompt and rating bar improved
milestones.summary: Kept the prompt visible during review and fixed the bottom rating bar around 1 Again, 2 Hard, 3 Good, and 4 Easy.

milestones.title: Learner-facing review card sections added
milestones.summary: Added Notes and Example sections to review cards, hid internal authoring/classification notes from learner-facing output, and kept useful compact linguistic notes available.

milestones.title: Example sentence correctness identified as next priority
milestones.summary: Next priority: fix Example sentence/source selection across language review decks so each non-exempt core review card gets one to three literal source/read-content examples where expected; Chinese conversion-only pronunciation decks remain exempt, and Japanese remains special until generated review decks are ready.

milestones.title: Latest pre-alpha devlog drafted
milestones.summary: Drafted the devlog post titled WackSmacker, Schmeckblacker, GladPecker, or something that actually sells? and noted that the current public build is still pre-alpha, not stable.

milestones.title: Korean Core renumbered around Introduction to Hangul
milestones.summary: Hangul writing-system material became Introduction to Hangul, and Korean Core now starts at the first sentence-pattern chapter.

milestones.title: Korean review decks split into smaller milestones
milestones.summary: Active Korean Core decks are Chapter 1-5, Chapter 6-10, and Chapter 11-15.

milestones.title: Chinese display name changed to Chinese - Mandarin
milestones.summary: Package-facing and menu-facing naming now leaves room for future Chinese - Cantonese and Chinese - Classical curricula.

milestones.title: Chinese Pinyin-Zhuyin decks created
milestones.summary: Chinese - Mandarin has Pinyin-Zhuyin and Pinyin-Zhuyin with Tones conversion review decks.

milestones.title: Vietnamese cast, Foundation, Core Chapters 1-5, and first deck created
milestones.summary: Vietnamese now has a fixed cast, Foundation chapters, Core Chapters 1-5, and the Chapter 1-5 review deck.

milestones.title: Package flow validates Korean, Chinese - Mandarin, and Vietnamese review sources
milestones.summary: WhackSmacker package generation, catalogue, installation, and review-source listing work across the current language package set.

milestones.title: Review continuation prompt added
milestones.summary: After finishing one package review source, WhackSmacker can offer to continue with the next deck.
